# ARCHITECTURE: Inventra
Generated by: Forge Architect Agent  
Version: 1.0.0 | Date: May 19, 2026

---

## System Overview

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  CLIENT LAYER                                           │
│  React Web App (Vite + React 18, mobile-first)         │
│  Hosted: Vercel                                         │
└─────────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────┘
                              │ HTTPS
┌─────────────────────────────▼───────────────────────────┐
│  API LAYER                                              │
│  Supabase (PostgreSQL + Auth + Realtime)                │
│  Row Level Security for multi-tenant isolation          │
└─────────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────┘
                              │
┌─────────────────────────────▼───────────────────────────┐
│  DATA LAYER                                             │
│  PostgreSQL (via Supabase)                              │
│  Tables: products, categories, stock_movements, users   │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

---

## Technology Stack

| Layer | Technology | Version | Justification |
|-------|-----------|---------|---------------|
| Frontend | React | 18.3.1 | Team knows it, large ecosystem |
| Routing | React Router | 6.26.0 | Standard, file-based routing |
| Styling | Tailwind CSS | 3.4.0 | Fast iteration, consistent tokens |
| State | React Context + hooks | built-in | No Redux needed for this scale |
| Backend | Supabase | latest | Auth + DB + API in one, no backend code |
| Testing | Vitest + RTL | latest | Fast, Vite-native |
| Build | Vite | 5.x | Fast HMR, excellent DX |
| Icons | Lucide React | 0.400.0 | Tree-shakeable, consistent |
| Dates | date-fns | 3.6.0 | Tree-shakeable, no moment.js |

---

## Forge 8-Layer Map

### Layer 1 — Config
```
constants.js    → APP_NAME, PAGINATION, DEBOUNCE_MS, STOCK_THRESHOLDS
routes.js       → ROUTES.DASHBOARD, ROUTES.INVENTORY, ROUTES.CATEGORIES, ROUTES.MOVEMENTS
theme.js        → All design tokens (colors, spacing, typography)
permissions.js  → ROLES.OWNER (single role in v1)
api.js          → SUPABASE_URL, SUPABASE_ANON_KEY, table names
features.js     → FEATURE_EXPORT_CSV: false (Phase 2)
env.js          → VITE_SUPABASE_URL, VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY
```

### Layer 2 — Types
```
common.types.js     → SortDirection, PaginationMeta, ApiResponse
product.types.js    → Product, ProductStatus (active/inactive/archived)
category.types.js   → Category
movement.types.js   → StockMovement, MovementType (sale/restock/adjustment)
```

### Layer 3 — Data (sample data for development)
```
products.data.js    → 25 clothing products, 3 edge cases (zero stock, archived)
categories.data.js  → 8 categories (tops, bottoms, dresses, etc.)
movements.data.js   → 50 stock movements across last 30 days
```

### Layer 4 — Utils
```
formatters.js   → formatCurrency (IDR), formatDate, formatRelativeTime
calculators.js  → calcStockStatus, calcInventoryValue, calcLowStockCount
validators.js   → validateProductName, validateSKU, validateStock, validatePrice
dateHelpers.js  → formatRelative, isToday, daysBetween
arrayHelpers.js → filterByQuery, sortArray, paginate, groupByCategory
colorHelpers.js → getStockStatusColor, getCategoryColor
```

### Layer 5 — Hooks
```
useFilter.js        → filter + search + sort any list
usePagination.js    → paginate any list
useDebounce.js      → 300ms debounce for search
useLocalStorage.js  → persist sidebar state, theme preference
useMediaQuery.js    → mobile breakpoint detection
useToast.js         → toast notifications
useModal.js         → modal state management
useForm.js          → form state + validation
useAsync.js         → async operation state
useClickOutside.js  → close dropdowns on outside click
useReducedMotion.js → animation preference
```

### Layer 6 — Services
```
api.service.js      → Supabase client wrapper with error handling
auth.service.js     → signIn, signOut, getSession, onAuthStateChange
product.service.js  → fetchAll, fetchById, create, update, delete, bulkUpdateStatus
category.service.js → fetchAll, create, update, delete
movement.service.js → fetchByProduct, recordSale, recordRestock, recordAdjustment
```

### Layer 7 — Components

**Tier A (ui/):** Button, Input, Textarea, Select, Checkbox, Badge, Card, Avatar, Modal, Drawer, Tooltip, Dropdown, Tabs, Accordion, Table, Pagination, Progress, Skeleton, Spinner, Alert, Toast, EmptyState, ErrorState

**Tier B (layout/):** AppShell, Sidebar, SidebarGroup, SidebarItem, TopBar, MobileNav, PageHeader, PageContainer

**Tier C (shared/):** KPICard, DataTable, FilterBar, SearchInput, StatusBadge, ConfirmDialog, FormModal, ExportButton (disabled in v1)

### Layer 8 — Features
```
dashboard/    → KPI overview: total products, low stock count, today's movements
inventory/    → Product list, add/edit/delete products, stock adjustment
categories/   → Category management
movements/    → Stock movement history with filters
settings/     → Account settings, profile
```

---

## Data Models

### Product
```typescript
interface Product {
  id:           string          // UUID v4
  userId:       string          // Owner's user ID (RLS)
  name:         string          // max 120 chars
  sku:          string          // unique per user, max 50 chars
  categoryId:   string | null   // FK to categories
  price:        number          // IDR, integer (no decimals)
  stock:        number          // current quantity, min 0
  reorderPoint: number          // alert threshold, default 5
  status:       ProductStatus
  notes:        string | null   // max 500 chars
  createdAt:    string          // ISO 8601
  updatedAt:    string          // ISO 8601
}

const ProductStatus = Object.freeze({
  ACTIVE:   'active',
  INACTIVE: 'inactive',
  ARCHIVED: 'archived',
})
```

### StockMovement
```typescript
interface StockMovement {
  id:          string
  userId:      string
  productId:   string          // FK to products
  type:        MovementType
  quantity:    number          // positive = increase, negative = decrease
  stockBefore: number          // stock before this movement
  stockAfter:  number          // stock after this movement
  note:        string | null   // max 200 chars
  createdAt:   string
}

const MovementType = Object.freeze({
  SALE:       'sale',
  RESTOCK:    'restock',
  ADJUSTMENT: 'adjustment',
})
```

---

## API Contract (Supabase RPC + Table API)

```
Products:
  GET    /rest/v1/products?select=*&order=updated_at.desc
  POST   /rest/v1/products
  PATCH  /rest/v1/products?id=eq.{id}
  DELETE /rest/v1/products?id=eq.{id}

Stock Movements:
  GET    /rest/v1/stock_movements?product_id=eq.{id}&order=created_at.desc
  POST   /rest/v1/stock_movements  (also updates product.stock via trigger)

All responses follow: { data: T | null, error: string | null }
Row Level Security: user can only access their own data (userId = auth.uid())
```

---

## Data Flow Diagrams

### Record a Sale
```
User taps "Record Sale" on ProductCard
    ↓
InventoryPage.jsx — calls handleRecordSale(productId, quantity)
    ↓
useInventory.js — recordSale(productId, quantity)
    ↓ Optimistic: update product.stock immediately in local state
    ↓
movementService.recordSale(productId, quantity)
    ↓
Supabase: INSERT into stock_movements + UPDATE products.stock (via trigger)
    ↓ Success
useInventory.js — confirms optimistic update (no change needed)
    ↓
Toast: "Sale recorded. Stock: 47 → 46"

ERROR PATH:
Supabase returns error (e.g., stock would go negative)
    ↓
movementService returns { data: null, error: "Insufficient stock" }
    ↓
useInventory.js — reverts optimistic update
    ↓
Toast error: "Cannot record sale. Insufficient stock."
```

---

## Build Sequence

```
Phase 0 (Day 1):    config/ + types/ + utils/formatters + styles/tokens.css
Phase 1 (Day 1-2):  utils/calculators + data/*.data.js
Phase 2 (Day 2-3):  components/ui/* (Button, Input, Modal, Skeleton, Toast)
Phase 3 (Day 3-4):  context/ + components/layout/* + App.jsx
Phase 4 (Day 4-5):  components/shared/* (DataTable, KPICard, StatusBadge)
Phase 5 (Day 5-7):  features/inventory/ (core feature)
Phase 6 (Day 7-8):  features/dashboard/
Phase 7 (Day 8-9):  features/categories/
Phase 8 (Day 9-10): features/movements/
Phase 9 (Day 10):   features/settings/ + auth flow
Total: ~10 days for MVP
```

---

## Architecture Decision Records

### ADR-001: Supabase over custom backend
**Status:** Accepted  
**Context:** Need auth + database + API. Team is 1 developer.  
**Decision:** Use Supabase (managed PostgreSQL + Auth + REST API).  
**Positive:** No backend code to write, auth built-in, RLS for security.  
**Negative:** Vendor lock-in, limited query flexibility.  
**Rejected:** Custom Node.js API — too much overhead for v1.

### ADR-002: No native app in v1
**Status:** Accepted  
**Context:** Target users are on mobile but building native app doubles timeline.  
**Decision:** Mobile-first React web app. Works in mobile browser.  
**Positive:** Single codebase, faster to ship, easier to update.  
**Negative:** No push notifications, no offline mode.  
**Rejected:** React Native — too complex for v1 scope.

---

## Technical Risk Register

| # | Risk | Probability | Impact | Mitigation |
|---|------|-------------|--------|------------|
| 1 | Supabase RLS misconfiguration exposes user data | Medium | Critical | Test RLS policies with multiple test accounts before launch |
| 2 | Stock goes negative due to race condition | Low | High | Server-side trigger validates stock before update |
| 3 | App too slow on 4G in Indonesia | Medium | High | Code splitting, skeleton screens, < 100KB initial bundle |
| 4 | Users abandon during onboarding | High | High | Measure time-to-first-product, target < 5 minutes |

---

## Performance Budgets

| Metric | Target |
|--------|--------|
| Initial bundle (gzip) | < 100KB |
| Feature chunk (gzip) | < 50KB |
| FCP on 4G | < 1.5s |
| LCP on 4G | < 2.5s |
| CLS | < 0.1 |
| Lighthouse Performance | ≥ 90 |
